parts.text:
These tests require actual Google credentials and call real APIs. They're integration tests. The CI/CD environment does not have GOOGLE_CREDS env var set. The failures are because the tests need live credentials.

Let me check how other tests are structured and whether there's a way to mark these as ignored or use mocks.

<path>/Users/mseele/dev/sve/sve-backend/src/logic/secrets.rs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: //! Secret management abstraction for sve-backend.
2: //
3: // Provides a `SecretProvider` seam with two adapters:
4: // - `EnvSecretProvider` reads secrets from environment variables (local/dev).
5: // - `ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider` reads a consolidated secret from AWS
6: //   Secrets Manager, falling back to environment variables first so local dev
7: //   keeps working via `.env`. Caching is owned by the adapter, not a global.
8: 
9: use anyhow::{Result, anyhow};
10: use async_trait::async_trait;
11: use aws_config::{BehaviorVersion, Region};
12: use aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client;
13: use mini_moka::sync::Cache;
14: #[cfg(test)]
15: use mockall::automock;
16: use serde::Deserialize;
17: use std::{env, time::Duration};
18: 
19: const CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME: &str = "sve-backend";
20: 
21: /// Typed key for every secret the application reads.
22: /// Replaces raw `&str` dispatch so call sites and the provider
23: /// agree on the vocabulary at compile time.
24: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
25: pub(crate) enum SecretKey {
26:     DatabaseUrl,
27:     TaskApiKey,
28:     CaptchaSecret,
29:     GoogleCreds,
30:     EmailAccounts,
31:     SepaCreditorName,
32:     SepaCreditorIban,
33:     SepaCreditorId,
34:     SessionSecret,
35: }
36: 
37: impl SecretKey {
38:     pub(crate) fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
39:         match self {
40:             Self::DatabaseUrl => "DATABASE_URL",
41:             Self::TaskApiKey => "TASK_API_KEY",
42:             Self::CaptchaSecret => "CAPTCHA_SECRET",
43:             Self::GoogleCreds => "GOOGLE_CREDS",
44:             Self::EmailAccounts => "EMAIL_ACCOUNTS",
45:             Self::SepaCreditorName => "SEPA_CREDITOR_NAME",
46:             Self::SepaCreditorIban => "SEPA_CREDITOR_IBAN",
47:             Self::SepaCreditorId => "SEPA_CREDITOR_ID",
48:             Self::SessionSecret => "SESSION_SECRET",
49:         }
50:     }
51: }
52: 
53: #[async_trait]
54: #[cfg_attr(test, automock)]
55: pub(crate) trait SecretProvider: Send + Sync {
56:     async fn get(&self, key: SecretKey) -> Result<String>;
57: }
58: 
59: /// Reads secrets from environment variables only.
60: pub(crate) struct EnvSecretProvider;
61: 
62: #[async_trait]
63: impl SecretProvider for EnvSecretProvider {
64:     async fn get(&self, key: SecretKey) -> Result<String> {
65:         env::var(key.as_str()).map_err(|_| anyhow!("Missing env var: {}", key.as_str()))
66:     }
67: }
68: 
69: #[derive(Deserialize, Clone)]
70: struct Secrets {
71:     #[serde(rename = "DATABASE_URL")]
72:     database_url: String,
73:     #[serde(rename = "TASK_API_KEY")]
74:     task_api_key: String,
75:     #[serde(rename = "CAPTCHA_SECRET")]
76:     captcha_secret: String,
77:     #[serde(rename = "GOOGLE_CREDS")]
78:     google_creds: String,
79:     #[serde(rename = "EMAIL_ACCOUNTS")]
80:     email_accounts: String,
81:     #[serde(default, rename = "SEPA_CREDITOR_NAME")]
82:     sepa_creditor_name: String,
83:     #[serde(default, rename = "SEPA_CREDITOR_IBAN")]
84:     sepa_creditor_iban: String,
85:     #[serde(default, rename = "SEPA_CREDITOR_ID")]
86:     sepa_creditor_id: String,
87:     #[serde(rename = "SESSION_SECRET")]
88:     session_secret: String,
89: }
90: 
91: /// Reads the consolidated AWS secret, falling back to environment variables.
92: /// Owns its own cache and lazily-initialized AWS client.
93: pub(crate) struct ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider {
94:     env: EnvSecretProvider,
95:     cache: Cache<String, Secrets>,
96:     client: tokio::sync::OnceCell<Client>,
97: }
98: 
99: impl ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider {
100:     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
101:         Self {
102:             env: EnvSecretProvider,
103:             cache: Cache::builder()
104:                 .time_to_live(Duration::from_secs(900)) // 15 minutes
105:                 .build(),
106:             client: tokio::sync::OnceCell::new(),
107:         }
108:     }
109: 
110:     async fn client(&self) -> Result<&Client> {
111:         self.client
112:             .get_or_try_init(|| async {
113:                 let config = aws_config::defaults(BehaviorVersion::latest())
114:                     .region(Region::new(
115:                         env::var("AWS_REGION").unwrap_or_else(|_| "eu-central-1".to_string()),
116:                     ))
117:                     .load()
118:                     .await;
119:                 Ok::<Client, anyhow::Error>(Client::new(&config))
120:             })
121:             .await
122:     }
123: 
124:     async fn load_secrets(&self) -> Result<Secrets> {
125:         let secret_name = CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME.to_string();
126:         if let Some(secrets) = self.cache.get(&secret_name) {
127:             return Ok(secrets);
128:         }
129: 
130:         let client = self.client().await?;
131:         let resp = client
132:             .get_secret_value()
133:             .secret_id(CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME)
134:             .send()
135:             .await
136:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("AWS SecretsManager error: {e}"))?;
137: 
138:         let secret_string = resp
139:             .secret_string()
140:             .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Secret {} not found", CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME))?;
141: 
142:         let secrets: Secrets = serde_json::from_str(secret_string)
143:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to parse consolidated secret: {e}"))?;
144: 
145:         self.cache
146:             .insert(CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME.to_string(), secrets.clone());
147:         Ok(secrets)
148:     }
149: }
150: 
151: #[async_trait]
152: impl SecretProvider for ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider {
153:     async fn get(&self, key: SecretKey) -> Result<String> {
154:         if let Ok(val) = self.env.get(key).await {
155:             return Ok(val);
156:         }
157: 
158:         let secrets = self.load_secrets().await?;
159:         match key {
160:             SecretKey::DatabaseUrl => Ok(secrets.database_url),
161:             SecretKey::TaskApiKey => Ok(secrets.task_api_key),
162:             SecretKey::CaptchaSecret => Ok(secrets.captcha_secret),
163:             SecretKey::GoogleCreds => Ok(secrets.google_creds),
164:             SecretKey::EmailAccounts => Ok(secrets.email_accounts),
165:             SecretKey::SepaCreditorName => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_name),
166:             SecretKey::SepaCreditorIban => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_iban),
167:             SecretKey::SepaCreditorId => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_id),
168:             SecretKey::SessionSecret => Ok(secrets.session_secret),
169:         }
170:     }
171: }
172: 
173: #[cfg(test)]
174: mod tests {
175:     use super::*;
176: 
177:     #[tokio::test]
178:     async fn env_secret_provider_returns_value_when_set() {
179:         let key = SecretKey::CaptchaSecret;
180:         unsafe {
181:             std::env::set_var(key.as_str(), "test-captcha-secret");
182:         }
183:         let provider = EnvSecretProvider;
184:         let result = provider.get(key).await;
185:         unsafe {
186:             std::env::remove_var(key.as_str());
187:         }
188:         assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), "test-captcha-secret");
189:     }
190: }

(End of file - total 190 lines)
</content>
state.title: src/logic/secrets.rs

parts.text:
These two tests are live integration tests that hit the real Google Calendar API and require `GOOGLE_CREDS` env var plus real credentials. They fail in CI because creds aren't available. The `SecretProvider` trait is `automock`-able.

Best fix: mark these two tests as `#[ignore]` so they don't run in CI by default but can still be run locally with `cargo test -- --ignored`. This is the standard Rust convention for tests requiring live external services / credentials.

Let me apply that fix.

These two tests are live integration tests that hit the real Google Calendar API and require `GOOGLE_CREDS` (and `DATABASE_URL`-free live network). They fail in CI because credentials aren't available. The standard Rust fix is to mark them `#[ignore]` so CI skips them, while local dev can still run them with `cargo test -- --ignored`.

parts.text: The "SecretProvider seam" commit seems most relevant. Let me look at it.

state.output:
commit 9cb54ce42d331cfb975b5542f0548e42ae6435d9
Author: Michael Seele <mseele@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jul 17 20:37:47 2026 +0200

    feat: carve SecretProvider seam for testable secrets
    
    Introduce a SecretProvider trait with EnvSecretProvider and
    ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider adapters, replacing the global
    Lazy<Arc<Mutex<Option<Client>>>> with adapter-owned caching.
    
    - SecretKey enum replaces stringly-typed dispatch
    - CalendarClient and RealEmailSender encapsulate secrets access,
      reducing parameter threading across call sites
    - All former secrets::get(...) call sites now use the trait
    
    Closes #70

parts.text:
So before this commit, the tests used a global `calendar::appointments` which presumably read GOOGLE_CREDS from a global. Now they use `EnvSecretProvider` directly. Let me look at how CalendarClient::new works and how it loads creds, and how it worked before.

Let me check src/calendar.rs to understand.

state.output:
<path>/Users/mseele/dev/sve/sve-backend/src/calendar.rs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: use std::sync::Arc;
2: 
3: use anyhow::{Context, Result, anyhow};
4: use chrono::{Duration, Local, NaiveDate, NaiveDateTime, TimeZone, Utc};
5: use chrono_tz::Europe::Berlin;
6: use google_calendar3::{
7:     CalendarHub,
8:     api::{Channel, Event, EventDateTime, Scope},
9:     hyper_rustls,
10:     hyper_rustls::HttpsConnector,
11:     hyper_util,
12:     hyper_util::client::legacy::connect::HttpConnector,
13:     yup_oauth2,
14:     yup_oauth2::ServiceAccountKey,
15: };
16: 
17: use crate::{
18:     logic::secrets::{SecretKey, SecretProvider},
19:     models::Appointment,
20: };
21: 
22: /// Owns the Google Calendar client and the secret needed to build it.
23: /// Callers use this instead of threading `&dyn SecretProvider` everywhere.
24: #[derive(Clone)]
25: pub(crate) struct CalendarClient {
26:     secrets: Arc<dyn SecretProvider>,
27: }
28: 
29: impl CalendarClient {
30:     pub(crate) fn new(secrets: Arc<dyn SecretProvider>) -> Self {
31:         Self { secrets }
32:     }
33: 
34:     async fn calendar_hub(&self) -> Result<CalendarHub<HttpsConnector<HttpConnector>>> {
35:         let secret: ServiceAccountKey = self
36:             .secrets
37:             .get(SecretKey::GoogleCreds)
38:             .await
39:             .and_then(|creds| serde_json::from_str(&creds).map_err(anyhow::Error::from))
40:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("Error loading credentials: {e}"))?;
41: 
42:         let connector = hyper_rustls::HttpsConnectorBuilder::new()
43:             .with_native_roots()
44:             .unwrap()
45:             .https_only()
46:             .enable_http2()
47:             .build();
48: 
49:         let executor = hyper_util::rt::TokioExecutor::new();
50:         let auth = yup_oauth2::ServiceAccountAuthenticator::with_client(
51:             secret,
52:             yup_oauth2::CustomHyperClientBuilder::from(
53:                 hyper_util::client::legacy::Client::builder(executor.clone())
54:                     .build(connector.clone()),
55:             ),
56:         )
57:         .build()
58:         .await?;
59: 
60:         let client = hyper_util::client::legacy::Client::builder(executor).build(connector);
61: 
62:         Ok(CalendarHub::new(client, auth))
63:     }
64: 
65:     pub(crate) async fn renew_watch(
66:         &self,
67:         calendar_id: &str,
68:         id: &str,
69:         resource_id: &str,
70:     ) -> Result<()> {
71:         // now + 1 year
72:         let expiration = Utc::now().timestamp_millis() + (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 365);
73: 
74:         let hub = self.calendar_hub().await?;
75: 
76:         // stop the current watch (ignore errors if channel already expired)
77:         let request = Channel {
78:             id: Some(id.into()),
79:             resource_id: Some(resource_id.into()),
80:             ..Default::default()
81:         };
82:         let _ = hub.channels().stop(request).doit().await;
83: 
84:         // add a new watch
85:         let request = Channel {
86:             id: Some(id.into()),
87:             type_: Some("web_hook".into()),
88:             address: Some("https://backend.sv-eutingen.de/api/calendar/notifications".into()),
89:             expiration: Some(expiration),
90:             ..Default::default()
91:         };
92:         hub.events()
93:             .watch(request, calendar_id)
94:             .add_scope(Scope::Full)
95:             .doit()
96:             .await?;
97: 
98:         Ok(())
99:     }
100: 
101:     pub(crate) async fn appointments(
102:         &self,
103:         calendar_id: &str,
104:         max_results: i32,
105:     ) -> Result<Vec<Appointment>> {
106:         let hub = self.calendar_hub().await?;
107: 
108:         let local_datetime = Local::now().with_timezone(&Berlin).naive_local();
109:         let time_min = Utc.from_local_datetime(&local_datetime).unwrap();
110: 
111:         let (_, events) = hub
112:             .events()
113:             .list(calendar_id)
114:             .add_scope(Scope::EventReadonly)
115:             .max_results(max_results)
116:             .time_min(time_min)
117:             .order_by("startTime") //$NON-NLS-1$
118:             .single_events(true)
119:             .doit()
120:             .await?;
121: 
122:         let appointments = match events.items {
123:             Some(events) => events
124:                 .into_iter()
125:                 .enumerate()
126:                 .map(|(index, event)| {
127:                     let sort_index = index.try_into().with_context(|| {
128:                         format!("Error converting index {} into sort index", index)
129:                     })?;
130:                     into_appointment(event, sort_index)
131:                 })
132:                 .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?,
133:             None => vec![],
134:         };
135: 
136:         Ok(appointments)
137:     }
138: }
139: 
140: fn into_appointment(event: Event, sort_index: u32) -> Result<Appointment> {
141:     let start = event.start;
142:     let end = event.end;
143:     Ok(Appointment::new(
144:         event.id,
145:         sort_index,
146:         event.summary,
147:         event.html_link,
148:         event.description,
149:         into_date(&start, 0)?,
150:         into_date(&end, -1)?,
151:         into_date_time(start)?,
152:         into_date_time(end)?,
153:     ))
154: }
155: 
156: fn into_date(date: &Option<EventDateTime>, days_to_add: i8) -> Result<Option<NaiveDate>> {
157:     let option = match date {
158:         Some(value) => match value.date {
159:             Some(date) => date.checked_add_signed(
160:                 Duration::try_days(days_to_add.into())
161:                     .with_context(|| format!("Cannot create duration of {days_to_add} days."))?,
162:             ),
163:             None => None,
164:         },
165:         None => None,
166:     };
167:     Ok(option)
168: }
169: 
170: fn into_date_time(date: Option<EventDateTime>) -> Result<Option<NaiveDateTime>> {
171:     let option = match date {
172:         Some(value) => value.date_time.map(|s| s.naive_local()),
173:         None => None,
174:     };
175:     Ok(option)
176: }

(End of file - total 176 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
OK so before, the global `get()` would check env var first, then fall back to AWS Secrets Manager. So in CI, if `GOOGLE_CREDS` env var wasn't set, it would fall back to AWS Secrets Manager. But CI was reaching AWS somehow? Wait — the tests were passing in CI before. So either:
1. CI had GOOGLE_CREDS env var set, OR
2. CI had AWS credentials configured so it could fetch from Secrets Manager.

After the refactor, `EnvSecretProvider` only reads env vars — it does NOT fall back to AWS. So if CI relied on the AWS fallback (no GOOGLE_CREDS env var, but AWS creds present), the new `EnvSecretProvider` breaks that.

So the fix: in tests, use `ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider` instead of `EnvSecretProvider`, which does the env-then-AWS fallback. That's the actual regression — the test was switched to a provider that doesn't have the AWS fallback.

Let me revert my ignore edits and use ConsolidatedAwsSecretProviderinstead.

The regression: before, `secrets::get()` fell back to AWS Secrets Manager when the env var was missing. The refactor switched the tests to `EnvSecretProvider`, which only reads env vars — dropping the AWS fallback that CI relied on. The fix is to use `ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider` in the tests (it has the same env-then-AWS behavior as the old `get()`).

Let me revert my `#[ignore]` change and fix the actual regression.

state.title: src/logic/calendar.rs

parts.text:
Root cause: the `SecretProvider` seam refactor (9cb54ce) switched these tests from the old global `secrets::get()` (which fell back to AWS Secrets Manager) to `EnvSecretProvider`, which only reads env vars. CI doesn't have `GOOGLE_CREDS` set as an env var — it relied on the AWS fallback.

Fix: use `ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider` in the tests — it preserves the original env-then-AWS fallback behavior (`src/logic/secrets.rs:154`). `ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider::new()` and the struct are `pub(crate)`, and `EnvSecretProvider` is now unused in tests, matching pre-refactor behavior.
